zh.lifeSummary: 當Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz於1258年5月12日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德時，他的父親Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León36歲，母親Violante de Aragón21歲。他於1281年與María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1340年在西班牙利安被列為king。他去世於1295年4月25日在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多享年36歲，埋葬在玻利維亞桑塔 克魯斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz于1258年5月12日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德时，他的父亲Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León36岁，母亲Violante de Aragón21岁。他于1281年与María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1340年在西班牙利安被列为king。他去世于1295年4月25日在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多享年36岁，埋葬在玻利维亚桑塔 克魯斯。

nameMeanings.name: El
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written ឯម, unexplained.
Perhaps also an altered form of German   Ehm  .

variants.name: Ehm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto.
German: variant of Öhm (see Ohm ).

nameMeanings.name: valiente,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Alfredo, Roberto, Alfonso, Ana, Arnaldo, Jesus, Juan.
Spanish: nickname based on valiente ‘brave’.

nameMeanings.name: El
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written ឯម, unexplained.
Perhaps also an altered form of German   Ehm  .

variants.name: Ehm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto.
German: variant of Öhm (see Ohm ).

nameMeanings.name: Feroz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of   Fiero  , a surname of German origin.
Hungarian (Feró): from a pet form of the personal name Ferenc ‘Francis’.

variants.name: Fiero
meanings: Italian: variant of   Fierro  .
Americanized form of German Führer (see Fuhrer ). Compare Fero .

nameMeanings.name: Sancho
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain origin, perhaps from Latin sanctus ‘holy’. It will always be associated with Sancho Panza , Don Quixote's companion.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: el
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered short form of Terry or Terence , of recent origin. For the substitution of -l for -r, compare Hal .

nameMeanings.name: Bravo,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Manuel, Miguel, Angel, Rafael, Guadalupe.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from bravo ‘fierce, violent, or courageous’ (from Latin barbarus ‘barbarian, ruffian’).

variants.name: Brabo
meanings: Spanish: altered form of   Bravo  .

variants.name: Brau
meanings: traditional name from brân ‘raven’, now in wider use. In Welsh and Irish mythology, the name was borne by Bran the Blessed, a giant-sized god, the son of the sea god Mannannan Mac Lir. He had many heroic adventures before being mortally wounded in the foot with a poisoned spear while attempting to rescue his sister Branwen in Ireland. At his behest, his severed head was carried to the hill where the Tower of London now stands and buried there.

nameMeanings.name: rey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a nickname for someone with grey hair or a grey beard (from Old English græg). In Scotland and Ireland it has also been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, grey’, such as Mac Riabhaich.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: See  Grey .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Castilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfredo, Carlos, Raul, Sergio, Jesus, Jorge, Pablo, Ruben, Blanca, Elido, Francisco, Guillermo.
Spanish: habitational name from Castile (Spanish Castilla), a region in central Spain. The name derives from the many castles in the region. This surname is also found in southern France.
History: As an independent kingdom between the 10th and 15th centuries, Castile formed the largest power in the Iberian peninsula.

variants.name: Castiglia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Annamarie, Antonino, Benedetto, Carmine, Francesco, Gaetano, Gennaro, Giacomo, Giuseppe, Ignazio.
Italian: habitational name from Castile (Italian Castiglia) in central Spain (see Castilla ).

nameMeanings.name: León
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Derivative of   Leo  , from the oblique case. This form is common as a Jewish name, but recently has also acquired wider currency. The lion is an important symbol among Jews because of Jacob's dying pronouncement that ‘Judah is a lion's whelp’ (Genesis 49:9).

en.lifeSummary: When Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz was born on 12 May 1258, in Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spain, his father, Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León, was 36 and his mother, Violante de Aragón, was 21. He married María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla in 1281, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. In 1340, his occupation is listed as king in León, Spain. He died on 25 April 1295, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, at the age of 36, and was buried in Santa Cruz, Bolivia.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz est né le 12 mai 1258, à Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Violante de Aragón, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla en 1281, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. En 1340, sa profession de son indiquée est king à León, Espagne. Il est décédé le 25 avril 1295, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, à l’âge de 36 ans, et a été enterré à Santa Cruz, Bolivie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz am 12. Mai 1258 in Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León 36 und seine Mutter Violante de Aragón 21. Er hat 1281 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1340 ist sein Beruf als king in León, Spanien aufgeführt. Er starb am 25. April 1295 im Alter von 36 Jahren in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien und wurde in Santa Cruz, Bolivien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz è nato il il 12 maggio 1258 nel Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spagna, il suo padre, Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Violante de Aragón, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla nel 1281, nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1340, la sua occupazione è indicata come king nel León, Spagna. Morì il 25 aprile 1295, nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, all'età di 36 anni, e fu sepolto a Santa Cruz, Bolivia.

ja.lifeSummary: Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Ferozが1258年5月12日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンヴァラドリドで生まれた時，父Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y Leónは36歳，母Violante de Aragónは21歳であった。1281年にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドでMaría “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castillaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1340年，スペインレoンでの職業はkingと記録されている。彼は1295年4月25日にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドで36歳で死去し，ボリビアサンタ クルズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz(이)가 1258년 5월 12일 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 발라도리드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Violante de Aragón(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1281년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1340년, 그의 직업은 스페인 레o에서 king이었다. 그는 1295년 4월 25일 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 볼리비아 산타 크루즈에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz nasceu em 12 de maio de 1258, em Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Violante de Aragón, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla em 1281, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1340, sua profissão aparece como king em León, Espanha. Ele faleceu em 25 de abril de 1295, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, com 36 anos, e foi sepultado em Santa Cruz, Bolívia.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz родился 12 мая 1258 г. в местности валладолид, кастилла и леoн, Испания, его отцу по имени Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Violante de Aragón, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla в 1281 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. В 1340 году он работал по профессии king в местности леoн, Испания. Он умер 25 апреля 1295 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания в возрасте 36 лет и был похоронен в местности санта круз, Боливия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sancho IV el Bravo, rey de Castilla y León El valiente, El Feroz nació el 12 de mayo de 1258, en Valladolid, Castilla y León, España, su papá, Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Violante de Aragón, tenía 21 años. Se casó con María “La Grande” Alfonso de Molina y Meneses Nieta de Alfonso IX. Señora de Molina y Ucero, Infanta de León, Reina Regente de Castilla en 1281, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1340, su ocupación registrada era king en León, España. Murió el 25 de abril de 1295, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, a la edad de 36 años, y fue sepultado en Santa Cruz, Bolivia.
